name: David Rumelhart
key_contributions: Co-authored the seminal 1986 Nature paper "Learning representations by back-propagating errors" with Hinton and Williams
Popularized backpropagation as a practical training method for multi-layer neural networks
Co-edited "Parallel Distributed Processing" (1986) — the bible of connectionism
Developed the connectionist framework for understanding cognition
summary: David Rumelhart was a cognitive scientist whose 1986 Nature paper with Hinton and Williams, 'Learning representations by back-propagating errors,' revived neural network research after the devastation of the first AI winter. By demonstrating that backpropagation could train multi-layer networks to learn useful internal representations, Rumelhart helped rescue connectionism from the graveyard to which Minsky and Papert's 'Perceptrons' critique had consigned it. His co-edited volumes 'Parallel Distributed Processing' became foundational texts for an entire generation of neural network researchers.
